medications.indication_rationale: Regular simple analgesia controls burn pain; source gave ibuprofen 400mg + paracetamol 1000mg stat.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1000mg up to QID + ibuprofen 400mg TID with food (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: bland fragrance-free emollient / aloe vera gel
medications.indication_rationale: Soothes intact epidermis; apply a few times daily.

medications.drug_name: topical antibiotic (e.g. silver sulfadiazine) on intact superficial burn
medications.indication_rationale: Not needed on an intact-epidermis superficial burn; may irritate.

medications.drug_name: ice applied directly to the burn
medications.indication_rationale: Ice causes further (frostbite) injury; use cool running water, not ice.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Loosely cover with cling film / non-stick dressing for comfort for the first 24h; wash gently with lukewarm water and mild soap; do not apply ice.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: A loose cover shields hypersensitive nerve endings; ice worsens injury.

patient_education_points.point: This is a superficial (first-degree) burn — only the top layer of skin. Because there are no blisters, it needs simple pain relief and a bland moisturiser, not antibiotics or special creams. It heals in about 3–7 days.
Don't put ice on it (that causes more damage) — cool running water is right. A loose cling-film cover for the first day eases the stinging from air.
Go to ED if it blisters, weeps, turns white/leathery or numb, spreads, or if you develop fever or thick yellow pus.

follow_up_plan.interval: 3 days if worsening (burns can deepen in 24–48h)
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Blistering/deepening, spreading redness, fever, or pus — ED/GP.

must_include_items: Assess depth/severity and referral criteria (blisters, special site, TBSA, inhalation)
Simple analgesia + bland emollient; NO ice
Correct first-aid reinforcement (cool water, not ice)
acceptable_alternatives: Non-adherent dressing instead of cling film; paracetamol and/or NSAID
errors_of_omission: Not advising reassessment as burns evolve
errors_of_commission: Prescribing topical/oral antibiotics for an intact superficial burn
Under-calling a special-site or deeper burn
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires depth/severity + referral assessment, simple analgesia/emollient with no ice, and a deeper-burn safety-net.
